learning_outcomes: Identify and describe the principal components of the human immune system.
Differentiate between innate and acquired immunity.
Discuss the role of Phagocytes, T cells, B cells, Complement system, MHC, Immune receptors and Immune Signaling compounds in the immune response.
Explain the basic immunological concept behind vaccines, allergic reactions, autoimmune responses and Cancer.
Apply the concepts learned in the course to the study of the development of Covid-19 infections, treatment and vaccine development.
policies: Assignments must be submitted via the Blackboard Course Shell; email submissions without prior permission will not be graded.
Participation is assessed based on promptness in completing assignments and adherence to due dates. Attendance is based on the last date of access to the course as recorded by Blackboard.
Participation in Discussion Boards, Journals, or Assignments must be original work; material from other sources must be properly referenced.
Late assignments will be accepted until the last day of class, but students will lose 25% of the total point value of the assignment for every week they are late.
Students are expected to access the course at least once a week.

For reasonable accommodation when absent, students should communicate with the faculty within 48 hours of the absence and be willing to provide evidence upon request; longer notification is allowed with a doctor’s note.
For long-term absences from a class (more than a week), students need to address concerns with the Office of Students with Disabilities or the Office of Academic Affairs.

Maintain civility in the online environment by writing in digestible chunks, avoiding all uppercase letters, using emoticons/emojis sparingly, using humor carefully, assuming best intentions, respecting differences, and choosing kind language.
recommendations: Students should expect to spend between 9 and 10 hours per week working on the course.
